3 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

How AI coding tools helped me build a developer tools platform and a crypto data website from scratch.
You needed to understand servers, databases, frontend frameworks, deployment systems, SEO, and many other technical details.
But with the rise of AI coding assistants, the way we build websites is changing.
Recently, I built two independent websites with the help of AI tools.
One focuses on developer utilities.
The other focuses on cryptocurrency and financial market information.
This experience changed how I think about building projects on the open web.
Building the First Project: A Developer Tools Platform
For the first project, I wanted to build a simple and scalable online tools platform.
The idea was not to create a complicated SaaS product.
Instead, I wanted to build useful tools that solve specific problems and allow users to access them directly through search engines.
The website uses a lightweight static architecture:
• Astro
• Tailwind CSS
• Cloudflare Pages
• Static content generation
The reason for choosing this approach was simple.
A static website is fast, reliable, and easy to maintain.
Each tool has its own independent page with:
• A clear explanation
• Examples of usage
• Frequently asked questions
• Structured metadata for search engines
The goal is to create pages that are genuinely useful instead of simply chasing traffic.
You can see the project here:
https://codekithub.com
Building the Second Project: A Crypto Market Information Website
The second project focuses on cryptocurrency and financial market information.
The goal was not to build another trading platform.
Instead, I wanted to create a place where users can quickly understand market conditions, sentiment changes, and important data.
One of the tools I built is a Crypto Fear & Greed Index tracker.
It helps users quickly check the current market sentiment:
https://qiaobax.com/tools/fear-greed-index/
The source code is also publicly available:
https://github.com/wobuliangren/crypto-fear-greed-index
Making this project open source was an important decision.
Sharing the code allows other developers to learn, improve, and provide feedback.
What I Learned From Building With AI
The biggest lesson from these projects is that AI does not replace the builder.
It changes the role of the builder.
In the past, building a website required spending a lot of time writing code.
Now, AI coding assistants can help with many technical tasks.
But the most important decisions still require human thinking:
• Choosing the right problem
• Understanding user needs
• Designing a simple solution
• Improving based on feedback
AI can accelerate development, but deciding what to build is still the most valuable skill.
Building on the Open Web
These projects also reminded me that independent websites still have opportunities.
You do not always need a large team or a big budget.
A useful idea, consistent improvement, and the ability to use modern tools can create something valuable.
The open web is still a place where individual builders can experiment and create.
I will continue documenting the process of building these projects, including technical decisions, SEO experiments, and lessons learned along the way.
